title: [FIRRTL] BlackBoxReader: something going on with resource paths
body:
When there is an annotation like this:
```json
{"class": "firrtl.transforms.BlackBoxResourceAnno", "resourceId": "/example.v"}
```
The pass succeeds when using a relative path for the input fir file, e.g. `./test.fir`, but fails after removing the prefixed `./`, e.g. `test.fir`

This is the error message:
```sh
test.fir:4:13: error: cannot open input file '/example.v': No such file or directory
  extmodule Example :
            ^
test.fir:4:13: note: see current operation: "firrtl.extmodule"() ( {
}) {annotations = [{class = "firrtl.transforms.BlackBoxResourceAnno", resourceId = "/example.v"}], arg_attrs = [], portDirections = false, portNames = [], sym_name = "Example", type = () -> ()} : () -> ()
```

Some extra steps:
```scala
; test.fir
circuit Foo: %[[{"class":"firrtl.transforms.BlackBoxResourceAnno","resourceId":"/example.v", "target":"~Foo|Example"}]]
  module Foo:
    skip
  extmodule Example :
```

```sh
# create empty v
touch ./example.v
# error
./bin/firtool test.fir
# success
./bin/firtool ./test.fir
```
